text.title: reranking-diversity-mmr Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of reranking-diversity-mmr enforces.

rule.statement: λ (lambda) is an explicit config value, default 0.5; tune per domain.
rule.rationale: Implicit defaults differ across libraries and confuse cross-team comparison.

rule.statement: Candidates must carry their embedding; do not re-embed inside MMR.
rule.rationale: Re-embedding inside the loop blows latency.

rule.statement: Pool size N >= 2K; warn when N < 2K (MMR has too little to choose from).
rule.rationale: With N≈K, MMR collapses to identity.

rule.statement: Use cosine similarity for pairwise comparisons; do not mix scoring schemes.
rule.rationale: Mixing dot-product and cosine in the same loop produces nonsense.

rule.statement: Every MMR call logs λ, N (pool), and K (output) for offline analysis.
rule.rationale: Without logging, post-hoc tuning is impossible.

rule.statement: Skip MMR for factoid queries or when candidate pool < 2K.
rule.rationale: Stub rule referenced from 06-decision-tree.xml when none of the qualifying branches fire.
